hero.question: Will Israel strike 3 countries in 2026?
hero.prob_take: At 17%, the crowd is pricing this as a rare outlier event, basically betting Israel stays contained to current theaters and avoids opening three simultaneous fronts in the next 12 months. The +6.5 point surge signals new risk premia around Iran escalation or Hezbollah resurgence, but 17% still feels like it's underweighting the compounding chaos of a region where one miscalculation cascades into three.

daily_take.headline: Israel Strike Odds Spike 6.5 Points. Market Pricing In Regional Escalation.
daily_take.deck: The "Israel strikes 3 countries in 2026" market jumped from 10.5% to 17% in 24 hours on $1.9M volume, signaling fresh conviction about Middle East escalation. The move smells like real information, not noise. Smart money is buying the dip on this one, especially at 17%, which still feels low if you believe Iran retaliation cycles are heating up.
